target.name: Physical Intelligence
target.founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Black Forest Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $3.25B

data.narrative:
Physical Intelligence, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, and Brian Ichter, is valued at $5.6B post-money following $600M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), spanning robotics and frontier AI stages from Series A through Series C.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier applied to the $5.6B last post-money, yields $10.6B. Peer-median valuation across five same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables—Figure AI ($39.0B Series C), Skild AI ($14.0B Series C), Reflection AI ($8.0B Series A), Together AI ($3.3B Series A), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B Series B)—produces a median of $8.0B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $39.0B.

Triangulating these methods implies a valuation range of $6.8B (low) to $12.2B (high), with a median of $9.3B. The current $5.6B valuation sits below this range, suggesting modest upside to next-round pricing. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—particularly the $39.0B outlier (Figure AI)—and the limited comparable public market reference, which constrains confidence in sector-wide benchmarking.
